mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and signals policy accommodation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20-day returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than returns following FEDFUNDS increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold) signals an incipient labor market downturn that precedes broad earnings deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9 percent near cycle lows as of 2023-11
failure_condition: In triggered instances, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered-return is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional average return (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand weakness that is not captured by either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days because both consumption and residential investment components of GDP are decelerating together.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM decline and HOUST MoM decline occur in the same month
failure_condition: Joint-decline episodes produce positive 20-day forward returns more than 50% of the time, or the interaction term's marginal predictive contribution over either single-series signal is not significant (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion accompanied by a restrictive monetary stance, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals that tight policy is starting to bite on industrial output, foreshadowing weaker corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; the same INDPRO deceleration in an accommodative-rate regime is not predictive because supply softness is offset by policy support.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative while FEDFUNDS is above its trailing 24-month median
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.1665 as of asof t-2mo
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO deceleration under restrictive FEDFUNDS conditions fails to produce below-median 60-day forward returns, or if the conditional effect is statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional INDPRO effect (interaction coefficient p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation rises above a 4% threshold, markets price in expectations of further Federal Reserve tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation decelerates back below the threshold.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during periods with CPI YoY above 4% are not significantly lower than the unconditional sample mean (p > 0.05), or hit rate of negative returns in triggered periods falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market strength and consumer income support, which sustains near-term equity risk appetite and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 148424 thousand persons as of asof t-1mo
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM change and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or PAYEMS revisions (high rev-risk series) reverse the sign of the reported employment change in more than 30% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST while FEDFUNDS remains elevated above 4% indicates that high mortgage rates are actively suppressing housing demand, a leading indicator of broader consumer retrenchment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; in a low-rate regime the same HOUST decline reflects idiosyncratic supply constraints rather than demand destruction and is not predictive.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1319 thousand units as of asof t-2mo
regime_hint: high interest rate regime with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: HOUST declines during high FEDFUNDS periods fail to precede below-median 60-day forward returns, or the effect disappears when tested against a placebo low-rate subsample (difference not significant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of falling INDPRO combined with rising UNRATE within the same reporting window signals a synchronized supply-side and labor-market downturn consistent with an Okun's-law-type relationship, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week because both signals reinforce recessionary risk beyond what either indicates alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline coincides with UNRATE MoM increase
failure_condition: Joint INDPRO-down/UNRATE-up episodes produce positive 5-day forward returns more than 50% of the time, or the interaction effect adds no significant predictive power over the single-series baselines (incremental Rank IC < 0.02).
